title: Manga to release Renewal versions of D and R and EoE hah
display_title: Manga to release Renewal versions of D and R and EoE hah
wikitext:
[]: Manga to release Renewal versions of D&R and EoE (hah!)             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jul 23, 2006 12:17 pm Quite close to it anyway: http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/article.php?id=9238

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Sun Jul 23, 2006 12:30 pm Well I'll be

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sun Jul 23, 2006 12:30 pm Lovely :roll: I get to waste even more of my money on the whore that is Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by ThroneofDravaris » Sun Jul 23, 2006 1:01 pm Wow, so that Manga guy I talked to at WAICON last year wasn't lying when he said 'maybe sometime in the future'. I wonder if it won't suck.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sun Jul 23, 2006 2:22 pm ThroneofDravaris wrote: Wow, so that Manga guy I talked to at WAICON last year wasn't lying when he said 'maybe sometime in the future'. I wonder if it won't suck.

[]:             Quote Post by rei on your parade » Sun Jul 23, 2006 2:58 pm What do you think the chances are of getting some good extras on this release?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 23, 2006 3:04 pm Depending on which animator was responsible, sometimes the head actually looks fairly normal. The rest of her just looks like a guy in a suit. Takeshi Honda "Yeah, but, like, assraping the original Eva design meant that we could rotoscope everything from handicam footage of the boys beating the crap out of each other in the back lot with giant cardboard swords and stuff, and that made the animation better! (Plus, it gave me an excuse to engage Assistant Director Tsurumaki in brutal hand-to-hand combat. I won, BTW.)" Reichu "That doesn't change the fact that you assraped the design! :throttles Honda-san:"

[]:             Quote Post by Trowa_Ayanami » Sun Jul 23, 2006 6:04 pm Because of this news, I feel both happy and pissed off at the same time. Happy because Manga are actually going ahead with releasing the Renewal version of Death & Rebirth and End Of Evangelion. But pissed off because, once again, there is yet ANOTHER version of Evangelion released and I have to pay more money for it. Whatever. I assume they will be out next year since next year is the 10th anniversery of End Of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by tokenflipguy » Sun Jul 23, 2006 6:59 pm So it won't look like shite on my HDTV. OMG

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Sun Jul 23, 2006 8:42 pm Trowa_Ayanami wrote: Whatever. I assume they will be out next year since next year is the 10th anniversery of End Of Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sun Jul 23, 2006 8:56 pm Rei on your parade wrote: What do you think the chances are of getting some good extras on this release?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 23, 2006 9:24 pm Come to think of it, I hope they are NOT releasing a Renewal "Death & Rebirth", because there is no such thing. There IS, however, "Death" (and EoE, of course).

[]:             Quote Post by ThroneofDravaris » Sun Jul 23, 2006 11:21 pm Legend of the Past wrote: ThroneofDravaris wrote: Wow, so that Manga guy I talked to at WAICON last year wasn't lying when he said 'maybe sometime in the future'. I wonder if it won't suck.

[]:             Quote Post by anatrok » Mon Jul 24, 2006 1:29 am YAY! I'm happy i never bought the movies now.... i did rent them...and they did look oh so awful...but now...maybe...it'll look good! YAY! I'm happy..... I hope it's shiny like the Platinums... And I hope it's cheep too. Other then that, It's about fucking time! I'm tired of watching EOE on my 15 inch monitor.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Mon Jul 24, 2006 7:43 am Oh boy what wonderful "extras" will they add in. I've never bothered to buy Death and Rebirth, maybe I should now. Yippe skippee extras that are sure to be AWL fanwanking her heart out.

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Mon Jul 24, 2006 10:10 am At this rate I might as well wait until everything comes down in price before i start buying this and the Platinum series edition

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Mon Jul 24, 2006 10:22 am What irks me is that, by this point in time, we should be watching Eva in some sort of high-definition format rather than buying it *again* on DVD. Instead, we got two crappy hi-def formats, neither of which I think is going to make it (and rightly so). Will I buy these? Maybe. But I think I'm going to sell my current Manga DVDs right now while there's still a market for them.

[]:             Quote Post by the3rdchildren » Wed Jul 26, 2006 9:35 am I've said for a while the next Evangelion DVDs I purchase will be the super-expensive high definition set that'll inevitably come out somewhere in the world. Hopefully the quality will even be stepped up from Renewal, which itself looks quite amazing.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Jul 26, 2006 12:41 pm Figures they would announce this just a couple of months after I imported them from Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Wed Jul 26, 2006 8:18 pm Hey, at least it's better than me. I shelled out $32 including shipping for the current D&R/EoE 2-Pack from Manga. Looks like I'll be shelling out again for the revival version of EoE, because the image quality on the disc I have sucks.
summary:

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Sun Jul 23, 2006 12:30 pm Well I'll be

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 &raq
